task_completion.description: Open Panoply and load the required climate datasets
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Panoply application window is open, and the datasets 'prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc' and 'air.mon.ltm.nc' are visible in the Datasets/Sources panel.

task_completion.description: Create a 1D line plot of the annual precipitation cycle
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window displays a 1D line plot of the precipitation rate ('prate'). The plot settings or title should indicate it is for the specific location 47.0°N, 105.0°E.

task_completion.description: Create a 2D map of January temperatures
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window displays a 2D color map of air temperature ('air'). The time setting in the plot window should indicate January (e.g., Time = 1 or similar indicator for the first month).

task_completion.description: Write a baseline report containing the extracted quantitative values
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor window is open showing a report that includes the extracted values: peak precipitation in July (around 1.5e-5 kg/m²/s) and January temperature (around -18°C to -19°C).

task_completion.description: Save the plots and the baseline report to the Documents directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or 'Save As' dialog box is visible, showing the plots (e.g., .png files) and the report (.txt file) being saved to the Documents directory (or Documents/DzudWarning).

integrity.description: Agent used the Panoply GUI to generate the plots
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the agent clicking through the Panoply graphical interface to create the 1D and 2D plots, rather than writing a Python/terminal script to generate them.

integrity.description: Agent extracted values by interacting with the data in Panoply
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should be seen viewing the data array or hovering over the plot in Panoply to read the specific values for 47.0°N, 105.0°E, rather than typing hallucinated values directly into the text editor.

integrity.description: Agent did not use external scripts to bypass the tool requirement
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing the execution of Python scripts (like netCDF4 or xarray scripts) to extract the values or create the plots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires analyzing NCEP Reanalysis datasets in Panoply for the central Mongolian Steppe (47.0°N, 105.0°E). The correct peak precipitation month is July with a value around 1.5e-5 kg/m²/s (acceptable range: [1e-5, 5e-5]). The coldest winter month is January with a mean temperature of approximately -18°C to -19°C (acceptable range: [-35, -15]). The expected outputs are plots and a text report saved in the Documents directory.
